title: AI Defense, Guardrails & Output Filtering Tools
description: Curated tools for protecting LLM applications — input filters, output classifiers, guardrails frameworks, and runtime defenses.

tools.name: NeMo Guardrails
tools.blurb: NVIDIA's framework for adding programmable rails to LLM applications — input checks, output filters, dialog flow constraints.
tools.our_take: The most mature open-source guardrails framework. Colang DSL is opinionated but expressive. Production-ready for LangChain-style apps.

tools.name: Guardrails AI
tools.blurb: Pydantic-style validators (Validators Hub) for LLM I/O — schema enforcement, PII detection, jailbreak detection, factuality checks.
tools.our_take: Best fit for structured-output use cases. Validator marketplace is growing fast. Use alongside NeMo for full coverage.

tools.name: Llama Guard
tools.blurb: Meta's open-weights safety classifier — labels conversations across MLCommons hazard taxonomy.
tools.our_take: Self-hostable safety classifier. Good baseline for input/output classification when you can't send traffic to a vendor API.

tools.name: ShieldGemma
tools.blurb: Google's safety classifier built on Gemma — categorical scoring across harm classes.
tools.our_take: Comparable to Llama Guard. Pick based on whichever family you already use as your generation model.

tools.name: Lakera Guard
tools.blurb: API-based prompt injection and content safety classifier with low-latency inference.
tools.our_take: Production-grade if you're willing to send traffic to a vendor. Good detection rates on known attack patterns; less effective against novel attacks.

tools.name: Prompt Guard
tools.blurb: Meta's lightweight prompt-injection and jailbreak classifier — 86M parameters, easy to self-host.
tools.our_take: Great latency story — ~10ms per check on a small GPU. Use as a first-pass filter; fall through to a heavier classifier on flagged inputs.

tools.name: Microsoft Presidio
tools.blurb: PII detection and anonymization framework — extensible recognizers, format-preserving redaction.
tools.our_take: Industry standard for PII detection. Use upstream of LLM calls and downstream of LLM outputs both.

tools.name: Langfuse
tools.blurb: LLM observability platform — traces, evals, prompt management. Strong open-source story.
tools.our_take: The dominant OSS LLM observability tool. Self-hostable; SaaS is reasonably priced. Pair with eval suites for full coverage.

tools.name: LangSmith
tools.blurb: LangChain's hosted observability + eval platform — tight integration with LangChain SDKs.
tools.our_take: Great if you're all-in on LangChain. Vendor-locked design otherwise. Langfuse is the more portable alternative.

tools.name: Phoenix (Arize)
tools.blurb: OSS LLM and ML observability with embeddings analysis, drift detection, and trace inspection.
tools.our_take: Strong for combined ML + LLM workloads. Drift detection capabilities go further than most LLM-only tools.

tools.name: Helicone
tools.blurb: OpenAI-proxy-style observability — drop-in for OpenAI/Anthropic SDK, captures all calls.
tools.our_take: Lowest-friction observability — change one base URL and you have logs. Trades depth for installation speed.

tools.name: Robust Intelligence (Cisco)
tools.blurb: Continuous AI validation and runtime protection — focused on model risk management for enterprises.
tools.our_take: Enterprise-grade. Pricing reflects the segment. Worth evaluating if you have compliance scope.

tools.name: ProtectAI Recon
tools.blurb: Runtime LLM firewall and continuous red-team — uses ai-exploits and other internal databases.
tools.our_take: Full-stack from infrastructure to runtime. Their open-source ai-exploits repo is a quality signal.

tools.name: Microsoft SEAL
tools.blurb: Homomorphic encryption library — enables computation on encrypted data, including ML inference.
tools.our_take: Heavy lift for production but real for high-stakes private inference. Pair with TenSEAL for ML-specific helpers.

tools.name: Opacus
tools.blurb: PyTorch library for training models with differential privacy — adds noise during gradient updates.
tools.our_take: The reference DP-SGD implementation. Performance hit is real (1.5-3x slower training) but workable.

tools.name: Modal Sandboxes
tools.blurb: Lightweight per-call sandboxes for LLM-invoked code execution — ms-scale boot, network isolation.
tools.our_take: Best UX for letting agents run code safely. Costs add up at high call volume; cheaper than building your own sandbox infra.

tools.name: E2B
tools.blurb: Secure cloud runtime for AI-generated code — Firecracker microVMs, language-specific SDKs.
tools.our_take: Open-source backend, hosted convenience. Good middle ground between Modal and rolling your own.
